dataset.title: Vegetation surveys on Peatland Action sites across Scotland in 2014, 2015 and 2021

abstract.para: These data were collected to assess the vegetation response to Peatland Action restoration activities across Scotland; at 16 sites during 2014-2015 and 13 (of these) sites in 2021. A total of 1,002 quadrats (mostly 2x2 m) were surveyed during 2014-2015 and 873 in 2021. Percentage cover of all living vascular and non-vascular species was estimated. The layers of vegetation were taken into account ñ such as the cover of non-vascular plants (bryophytes and lichens) and layers of vascular plants above this. This means that the percentage cover of vegetation could add up to more than 100%. Where factors such as bare ground are present, the vegetation coverage may also add up to less than 100% of the quadrat. Sample areas were either pre-restoration areas, post-restoration areas, unrestored control areas or unmodified reference areas.

intellectualRights.para: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution (CC-BY) 4.0 License.

purpose.para: These data were collected to assess the vegetation response to peatland restoration at Peatland Action sites across Scotland.

description.para: The general approach to the surveys, data management and processing, and statistical analysis, together with specific details of how the survey was carried out at each site including site-specific constraints, are outlined in the supporting reports (Haycock & Cashon 2022; Ross & Blackshaw 2022).

gbif.citation: NatureScot (2025). Vegetation surveys on Peatland Action sites across Scotland in 2014, 2015 and 2021. Occurrence dataset https://doi.org/10.15468/7yz8f2 accessed via GBIF.org on 2026-05-22.
